originalTitle: TechnipFMC and Valaris Shares Plummet, What You Need To Know
summary: A number of stocks fell in the afternoon session after crude oil dropped to its lowest level since the start of the Iran war, as tankers resumed transit through the Strait of Hormuz and the U.S. and Iran signaled progress toward ending the conflict.
content:
A number of stocks fell in the afternoon session after crude oil dropped to its lowest level since the start of the Iran war, as tankers resumed transit through the Strait of Hormuz and the U.S. and Iran signaled progress toward ending the conflict.

The S&P 500 energy index fell about 2.45%, the weakest major sector even as the broader market held roughly flat. Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) each fell in the ~2–2.5% range (exact figures vary by source). The more oil-price-sensitive explorers and producers were hit harder as Occidental (OXY), ConocoPhillips (COP), Devon (DVN) and APA Corp all fell roughly 2.5–3.5%. Oilfield-services names (Halliburton, SLB) and refiners (Valero, Phillips 66, Marathon Petroleum) slipped about 1.5–2.5%.WTI fell about 4% to near $70 and Brent about 4% to near $74,the lowest since February 27, the day before U.S.–Israeli strikes on Iran, leaving crude down roughly 40% from its wartime peak.

The driver was physical and visible: tankers openly crossing Hormuz with transponders on, the IMO citing safety guarantees, and the IEA estimating the UAE exporting near 85% of pre-war levels. Separately, Trump ordered a DOJ probe into why pump prices "haven't fallen faster," accusing oil companies of gouging.

The stock market overreacts to news, and big price drops can present good opportunities to buy high-quality stocks.

Among others, the following stocks were impacted:

Oilfield Services company TechnipFMC(NYSE:FTI) fell 3.1%.Is now the time to buy TechnipFMC? Access our full analysis report here, it's free.

Oilfield Services company Valaris(NYSE:VAL) fell 3.7%.Is now the time to buy Valaris? Access our full analysis report here, it's free.

Valaris's shares are very volatile and have had 25 moves greater than 5% over the last year. In that context, today's move indicates the market considers this news meaningful but not something that would fundamentally change its perception of the business.

The previous big move we wrote about was 6 days ago when the stock dropped 6.4% on the news that the U.S. and Iran signed an interim agreement that would waive sanctions on Tehran's oil and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

WTI futures fell as much as 3.5% to an intraday low of $73.60, the lowest since March 2, the first trading day after the initial US-Israeli strikes on Iran, while Brent crude dropped 2% to $77.96.The catalyst was a 14-point memorandum of understanding signed by the US and Iran, which begins a 60-day negotiation period. This stripped away the geopolitical risk premium that had been the energy sector's most powerful tailwind for months. Under its terms, Iran will allow toll-free passage through the Strait of Hormuz immediately, with full traffic capacity restored within 30 days. Roughly 20% of the world's seaborne oil and LNG transits the strait. Saudi tankers and LNG carriers were already departing the Gulf region as shipping activity began to normalize.

Oil reached as high as $120 per barrel at the peak of the conflict and fell nearly 29% in a month. That collapse reflects markets pricing in the return of Iranian barrels to global supply, barrels that had been sanctioned out of the market, alongside the reopening of the world's most critical energy shipping lane.

Valaris is up 44.1% since the beginning of the year, but at $75.17 per share, it is still trading 33.7% below its 52-week high of $113.42 from May 2026. Investors who bought $1,000 worth of Valaris's shares 5 years ago would now be looking at an investment worth $2,564.

chineseTitle: TechnipFMC 和 Valaris 股价暴跌，您需要知道什么
chineseSummary: 随着油轮恢复通过霍尔木兹海峡以及美国和伊朗在结束冲突方面取得进展，原油跌至伊朗战争爆发以来的最低水平，许多股市在下午时段下跌。
chineseContent:
随着油轮恢复通过霍尔木兹海峡以及美国和伊朗在结束冲突方面取得进展，原油跌至伊朗战争爆发以来的最低水平，许多股市在下午时段下跌。

标准普尔 500 能源指数下跌约 2.45%，是最弱的主要板块，尽管大盘基本持平。埃克森美孚 (XOM) 和雪佛龙 (CVX) 的跌幅均在 2-2.5% 左右（确切数字因来源而异）。对石油价格更加敏感的勘探商和生产商受到的打击更大，西方石油公司 (OXY)、康菲石油公司 (COP)、德文郡 (DVN) 和 APA 公司均下跌约 2.5-3.5%。油田服务公司（Halliburton、SLB）和炼油商（Valero、Phillips 66、Marathon Petroleum）下跌约 1.5-2.5%。WTI 下跌约 4%，至 70 美元附近，布伦特原油下跌约 4%，至 74 美元附近，为 2 月 27 日（美以袭击伊朗的前一天）以来的最低水平，原油价格较战时峰值下跌约 40%。

司机是有形的、可见的：油轮在打开应答器的情况下公开穿越霍尔木兹海峡，国际海事组织援引安全保证，国际能源署估计阿联酋的出口量接近战前水平的 85%。另外，特朗普下令司法部调查为何油价“没有更快下跌”，并指责石油公司欺诈。

股市对消息反应过度，价格大幅下跌可能是购买优质股票的好机会。

其中，以下股票受到影响：

油田服务公司TechnipFMC（纽约证券交易所股票代码：FTI）下跌3.1%。现在是买入TechnipFMC的时候吗？在这里访问我们的完整分析报告，它是免费的。

油田服务公司 Valaris (NYSE:VAL) 下跌 3.7%。现在是买入 Valaris 的时候吗？在这里访问我们的完整分析报告，它是免费的。

Valaris 的股价波动很大，去年有 25 次涨幅超过 5%。在这种背景下，今天的举动表明市场认为这一消息有意义，但不会从根本上改变其对该业务的看法。

我们之前写过的大动作是在 6 天前，当时有消息称美国和伊朗签署了一项临时协议，将放弃对德黑兰石油的制裁并重新开放霍尔木兹海峡，导致该股下跌 6.4%。

WTI 期货跌幅高达 3.5%，至盘中低点 73.60 美元，为 3 月 2 日以来的最低水平，即美国和以色列首次对伊朗发动袭击后的第一个交易日，而布伦特原油则下跌 2%，至 77.96 美元。催化剂是美国和伊朗签署的 14 点谅解备忘录，该备忘录开始了为期 60 天的谈判期。这消除了地缘政治风险溢价，而地缘政治风险溢价多年来一直是能源行业最强大的推动力。根据协议条款，伊朗将立即允许霍尔木兹海峡免费通行，并在30天内恢复全部通行能力。世界上大约 20% 的海运石油和液化天然气经过该海峡。随着航运活动开始正常化，沙特油轮和液化天然气运输船已经离开海湾地区。

油价在冲突最高峰时曾高达每桶120美元，一个月内下跌近29%。这种暴跌反映出市场定价是伊朗石油恢复全球供应，这些石油已被禁止进入市场，同时世界上最重要的能源运输通道也重新开放。

自今年年初以来，Valaris 股价上涨了 44.1%，但以每股 75.17 美元的价格计算，仍比 2026 年 5 月以来的 52 周高点 113.42 美元低 33.7%。五年前购买了价值 1,000 美元的 Valaris 股票的投资者现在将考虑价值 2,564 美元的投资。

当你在这里时：下一个 Palantir？一家卫星公司捕获地球上每个点的图像。每一天。五角大楼想要它。对冲基金正在利用它来超越收益。你可能从未听说过它。

这就是 Palantir 在成为价值 4370 亿美元的巨头之前的早期样子。同样的剧本。不同的技术。如果你错过了 Palantir，你需要看看这个。在此免费索取股票行情。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
